scene.description: Citizen R-440's recurring dream, filed as memory. A corridor in the Bureau that doesn't exist on any floor plan. The subject walks it every night. So, apparently, do you.

fragments.label: The Corridor
fragments.text: A hallway longer than the building that contains it. Doors on both sides, all unnumbered, all warm to the touch.

fragments.label: The Repetition
fragments.text: The subject has had this dream 1,114 times. The count matches a case number you handled. You don't remember handling it.

fragments.label: The One Open Door
fragments.text: One door stands open. Inside is the dive you just left — the missing-record street, frozen mid-panic. The dream contains your last case.

fragments.label: The End of the Hall
fragments.symbol: At the corridor's end, where a window should be: a reflective surface showing the room you're physically sitting in right now, examining this memory.

interpretations.label: File it as a dream — it isn't real
File it as memory — it happened
Refuse the distinction — dream and record are the same data
